Asset Can Execute? Notes
Core Activations
1 High-visibility brand placements
Strategic MyPrize branding on the exterior ring canvas, venue entryways, and banners in high-traffic areas.
✓ YesCanvas placement, exterior cage LED, venue LED screens
2 Interactive on-site booth
Dedicated staffed space (tent, banner, desk, swag) to guide fans through the platform. Fans receive 5–10 Sweeps Cash to play on-site and redeem winnings (if possible). Content can be filmed here.
✓ YesPFL to scope available spaces and requirements.
3 In-arena audio promos
Live announcer shoutouts integrated with an exclusive, event-specific signup code to drive immediate acquisitions.
✓ Yes—
4 “Big check” moments
Oversized checks for highly shareable in-ring / post-fight photo ops (as done in Sioux Falls).
✓ YesCan replicate the previous backstage cheque handout.
5 TV-network reach
PFL is broadcast live on ESPN / ESPN2 / ESPN+. Opportunity to leverage this reach.
✓ Yes—
6 Winning fighter presented by MyPrize
Winning fighter presented by MyPrize on broadcast / in-arena.
✓ YesESPN blocks social-casino / sweepstakes integrations, but the announcer can mention MyPrize as presenter of selected fight(s), with an in-arena readout.
7 Signup QR code on TVs
MyPrize signup QR code displayed on venue TV screens.
✓ YesESPN blocks integrations on broadcast, but we can use in-arena LED screens that are visible on-screen.
8 Seat upgrade
Download MyPrize at the event for a chance to win cage-side seats — “with MyPrize, you get in the action.”
✓ YesPossible via concourse activation space and/or LED screen involvement.
Content Opportunities
9a Mic’d up fighters
Further behind-the-scenes access via mic’d up fighters.
✓ YesAs done previously with post-fight winner interviews and gameplay. Potential to add non-competing talent.
9b Between-fights contest
Crowd plays a game between fights; winner receives a reward.
✓ YesTo be scoped further — see 11c / 11d for direction.
9c Talent & interviews
Interviews with on-air talent: announcers, English-speaking fighters, coaches, ring girls. Priority on English-speaking fighters with no gaming conflict. Needs a designated PFL point person on event day.
✓ YesSame scope as previous events, with added interview opportunities during fight week.
9d Full event access
Pre-event (weigh-ins, walkouts, locker room) and post-fight (press conferences, backstage) access. Guerrilla-style filming of the host playing games mid-event.
✓ YesAll fight-week activations with talent (incl. ring girls). Announcers / commentators / host not available fight night, but all others are.
9e The hero moment
After a fighter wins (ideally post-knockout), he runs to the cage where the host waits with a phone, plays a round of blackjack and wins. Stake ran a version — reference available.
◐ PartialNot possible in this exact way due to existing partner assets. Can create multiple hero moments throughout the night — see 11c and 11d for more info.
Continue Existing Content Agreements

Continue Meta partner access.
✓ Yes—

PFL sends emails promoting MyPrize — 1× per event.
✓ Yes—
10c MyPrize creative team on-site
MyPrize sends a creative team (1 producer + 1 videographer / photographer) to each event.
✓ Yes—

11a PFL organic social posts
PFL posts about MyPrize on organic social feeds — 2× per event.
✓ YesNew addition — not in the initial agreement.

PFL posts about MyPrize on IG stories — 2× per event.
✓ YesNew addition — not in the initial agreement.
Hero Moments — Sponsored LED
11c Sponsored LED wristbands
Sponsored wearable wristbands for all attendees, turning the arena into a sea of MyPrize blue.
✓ YesFull examples and video in the linked microsite.▶ View LED microsite
11d Wristband activation on-site
3× moments throughout the night engaging someone in the audience for MyPrize promotions and branding.
✓ YesMoments could include co-branded merch wins, free site credit, or filming the fan playing MyPrize on-screen and winning.
